channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa Faces Challenges in Reducing Food Waste by 2030
item.description: South Africa discards about a third of its food production annually, roughly 10 million tonnes, while millions of its citizens face hunger. This situation highlights a paradox: the country has enough ...

item.title: Defence cites inconclusive ballistic evidence in Charlie Kirk murder case
item.description: Investigators in the murder of conservative activist Charlie Kirk are facing challenges in linking the fatal bullet to the rifle allegedly used by Tyler Robinson, according to defence documents filed ...

item.title: The King's Upcoming Visit to Washington Amid Tensions with President Trump
item.description: The King is preparing for a high-profile visit to Washington at a time when relations between the United Kingdom and the United States appear strained. The visit follows recent social media remarks fr...

item.title: How Cruises Simplify Travel and Help You Relax
item.description: Planning a vacation can often feel more stressful than rewarding. The process of coordinating flights, hotels, transfers, and schedules can quickly turn excitement into anxiety. Many travelers find th...

item.title: King Charles to proceed with US state visit despite concerns over Iran conflict and Trump tensions
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla are set to undertake a state visit to the United States from 27 to 30 April, marking the 250th anniversary of US independence. Buckingham Palace confirmed the visit will...

item.title: Queen Elizabeth II Garden Opens in Regent's Park as a Commemorative Space
item.description: The Queen Elizabeth II Garden in Regent's Park, London, is set to open to the public next month as a tribute to the late monarch. Conceived during her lifetime, the garden honours her lifelong love of...

item.title: National Treasury to centralise R88bn in unclaimed assets for better beneficiary tracing
item.description: The National Treasury is moving to address R88 billion in unclaimed assets held across banks, insurance companies, and retirement funds. Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana announced reforms that includ...

item.title: Kenya’s Gen Z Mobilises to Participate in Politics
item.description: Kenya’s Gen Z is making a strong statement in politics by actively registering to vote, signalling they are tired of just observing and are ready to author their own destiny. The Tuko Kadi movement ha...

item.title: Aviation fuel hits record ₹2.07 lakh per kilolitre, commercial LPG rates see significant hike
item.description: On April 1, 2026, India witnessed an unprecedented rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices, which more than doubled to ₹2.07 lakh per kilolitre, marking the first time in history that ATF has cross...

item.title: Iran Issues Warning Against US Tech Companies Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has issued a warning that it plans to target major American technology companies starting April 1, citing retaliation for US and Israeli military action...

item.title: Government announces top-level bureaucratic reshuffle, appoints new secretaries and DGCA chief
item.description: The Government of India announced a major bureaucratic reshuffle on Tuesday, involving several top-level appointments across ministries and departments. Chanchal Kumar, an IAS officer of Bihar cadre, ...

item.title: Volvo Moves All Polestar 3 Manufacturing to South Carolina to Streamline Production
item.description: Volvo and its electric performance brand Polestar have decided to relocate all production of the Polestar 3 SUV from China to the company’s plant in Charleston, South Carolina. The move ends manufactu...

item.title: UDA Alleges Uhuru Kenyatta is Supporting Opposition Against Ruto's Government
item.description: The United Democratic Alliance (UDA) has openly accused former President Uhuru Kenyatta of attempting to undermine President William Ruto’s administration. UDA Secretary General Hassan Omar claimed th...

item.title: King and Queen Will Not Meet Epstein Survivors During Upcoming US State Visit
item.description: The King and Queen of the United Kingdom are scheduled to undertake a state visit to the United States between 27 and 30 April, confirmed by former US President Donald Trump. Despite calls from US Con...

item.title: Insider Update on The Sims 4 Marketplace Success and Project X Development Adjustments
item.description: A recent insider report has shed light on current developments in The Sims franchise, particularly regarding The Sims 4 Marketplace and Project X, a new single-player Sims game in development by Maxis...

item.title: Nigerian Banks Raise Over N4 Trillion to Meet New Capital Requirements
item.description: Over the past two years, Nigerian banks don show say dem sabi handle money matters well, as dem don raise more than N4.6 trillion to meet the Central Bank of Nigeria's new capital requirements. Dis ex...

item.title: Fort Hare places vice-chancellor and three senior managers on precautionary suspension
item.description: The University of Fort Hare has placed its vice-chancellor, Prof Sakhela Buhlungu, on precautionary suspension following an investigation that found he and three other senior management staff failed t...

item.title: Iran attacks tanker near Dubai amid rising Middle East tensions
item.description: Early Tuesday, Iran attacked a Kuwait-flagged crude oil tanker, Al-Salmi, near Dubai, setting it on fire. This came after President Trump threatened to destroy Iran's energy facilities if the Strait o...

item.title: Trump administration admits Strait of Hormuz reopening may take longer than planned amid Iran conflict
item.description: The Trump administration is facing challenges in reopening the Strait of Hormuz as a prerequisite for declaring the war with Iran over. Sources inside the White House indicate that achieving both mili...

item.title: Romeo and Juliet West End production praised for lead performances despite heavy directorial style
item.description: The West End debut of Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe in Robert Icke’s contemporary production of Shakespeare’s Romeo and Juliet has been met with mixed reactions. While Icke’s interpretation demonstrates cr...
